title: Minimax shift-robust validation selection
summary: Official docs for grouped CV stress non-overlapping groups, and uncertainty literature shows performance/calibration degrade under shift.
rationale: Official docs for grouped CV stress non-overlapping groups, and uncertainty literature shows performance/calibration degrade under shift. With only 4 years and 26 races, mean CV can hide brittle race-specific overfit.
implementation_hint: Run a stress suite with at least two split families: `StratifiedGroupKFold` on `Year_Race` and leave-one-year-out or `GroupKFold` on `Year`. Pick features/hyperparameters by worst-fold or lower-quantile AUC, not only mean AUC. Use this as model-selection logic, not as a new model.
expected_effect: May slightly lower headline local CV while improving public-LB reliability and avoiding race/year leakage traps.
risk: Can reject genuinely strong but variable models; with few groups, variance of the estimate is itself high.
